design_philosophy.core_loop: Freeze enemies in snow, transform them into rolling snowballs, chain knockdowns for bonuses and progression through 50 stages
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Time pressure via invincible pumpkin head spawn if level takes too long; enemy avoidance while executing puzzle-like snowball chains
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Cooperative snowman rescue mission with physics-based enemy manipulation and risk-reward scoring mechanics
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Explicitly puzzle-platformer hybrid with mandatory boss encounters every 10 stages; rewards skill-based chain execution (one-snowball clears) over pure reflexes; time limit creates escalating threat (pumpkin → ghosts) forcing player pacing decisions

difficulty.lives_system: Lives-based with respawn at stage start on death; losing a life resets player firepower and speed modifiers to base state
difficulty.continues_system: Arcade continue system (credit insertion); NES version uses traditional continue mechanics
difficulty.checkpointing: Per-stage checkpoint at entrance; no mid-stage checkpoints
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Hidden bonus secrets are 'crucial for reaching high-scores to obtain extra lives,' suggesting skill-based progression rather than difficulty settings; four color-coded power-ups (speed, snow coverage, throw distance, flight) create tactical loadout variation

progression.upgrade_system: Temporary power-ups from defeated enemies: red (speed), blue (snow coverage), yellow (throw distance), green (flight/balloon mode); firepower/speed penalties persist across lives until regained from pickups
progression.unlock_mechanism: Boss gate every 10 stages; mandatory defeat to progress through 50-stage sequence
replayability_hooks: Hidden bonus secrets tied to high-score chasing
One-snowball clear bonus (all enemies eliminated by single rolling snowball chain)

world_building_techniques: Character identity via visual design (Nick blue overalls, Tom red overalls)
NES-specific narrative variation (King Scorch curse premise vs. arcade princess rescue framing)
Regional plot divergence acknowledged but not detailed in source

boss_design.philosophy: Mandatory progression gates at intervals (every 10th stage); suggests pattern-based encounter design tied to stage-based narrative pacing

faction_indicators: Twin protagonists (Nick/Tom) with distinct visual branding
Rescue mission objective (princesses Puripuri/Puchipuchi in arcade; Teri/Tina in NES)
danger_signals: Time-pressure invincible pumpkin head spawn mechanic (creates urgency escalation)
Ghost spawning from pumpkin (unseeable/unstunnable threats forcing evasion behavior)
environmental_storytelling: Stage-by-stage progression through 50 levels implies world traversal narrative
Power-up color coding provides immediate environmental feedback (red=speed, blue=coverage, yellow=range, green=flight)

sources_used: wikipedia: Snow Bros. (gameplay section, NES narrative variant, boss/progression structure, power-up system)
